data.title: Digital therapeutic RevitalVision shows promising improvement in visual function for infantile nystagmus patients, published data in British Journal of Ophthalmology showcases groundbreaking results.
data.description: Promising clinical results recently announced in the British Journal of Ophthalmology reveal a significant enhancement in visual function for patients with infantile nystagmus. The groundbreaking digital therapeutic showcased an impressive improvement in visual capabilities for affected individuals. This non-invasive treatment offers hope for enhancing visual function in patients with infantile nystagmus, potentially revolutionizing their quality of life.

data.title: Hexaware Technologies partners with AccuKnox to expand Zero Trust cloud security platform in enterprise accounts.
data.description: In Menlo Park, California, on January 13, 2026, AccuKnox announced a collaboration with Hexaware Technologies to enhance its Zero Trust cloud security platform for enterprise clients handling hybrid and multi-cloud environments. This partnership aims to address the increasing challenges posed by complex infrastructures in hybrid and multi-cloud setups.

data.title: Earnings for JPMorgan Chase: $4.92/share, $46.02B revenue Delta Air Lines: $1.53/share, $15.58B revenue. BNY Mellon also reporting.
data.description: Before the market opening, investors are anticipating earnings reports from key companies. JPMorgan Chase, listed on NYSE as JPM, is expected to disclose earnings of $4.92 per share with revenue hitting $46.02 billion. Delta Air Lines, listed on NYSE as DAL, is projected to post earnings of $1.53 per share on revenue of $15.58 billion. Additionally, Bank of New York Mellon, listed on NYSE as BK, is also set to reveal their latest figures. For more details, the complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Dehumidifier market to hit $5.53 bn by 2031 with a 7.21% CAGR, up from $3.9 bn in 2026 and $3.64 bn in 2025.
data.description: In Hyderabad, India, as of January 13, 2026, the dehumidifier market has seen significant growth. Based on Mordor Intelligence's recent findings, the market size has expanded from USD 3.64 billion in 2025 to approximately USD 3.9 billion in 2026. Projections indicate a further surge to USD 5.53 billion by 2031, showcasing a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 7.21%.

data.title: Euna Solutions acquires GrantExec to enhance AI-driven grants discovery and research for public sector, nonprofits.
data.description: Euna Solutions®, a prominent provider of specialized cloud solutions for the public sector, has revealed the acquisition of GrantExec, an innovative AI-native startup focused on streamlining grant discovery and research for non-profits, universities, and community-centric organizations. This acquisition enhances Euna's grants portfolio by enhancing its advanced AI-driven grant research features in the Euna Grants software suite.

data.title: Veteran strategist Ed Yardeni dismisses Trump's 10% credit card cap ultimatum as a "trial balloon" unlikely to become law.
data.description: Despite President Donald Trump's announcement of a potential 10% cap on credit card rates, strategist Ed Yardeni believes it is unlikely to become law, dismissing it as political noise. Yardeni spoke on CNBC about the decline in stocks of major banks like JPMorgan Chase & Co. (NYSE: JPM) and Capital One Financial Corp. (NYSE: COF) following Trump's ultimatum that rates must be lowered by the Jan. 20 inaugural anniversary. Yardeni views this as one of many administration "trial balloons" to gauge public reaction, stating that passing such a measure would require congressional legislation, and powerful banking lobbyists would likely prevent its approval. The strategist emphasized that the administration frequently tests various proposals to assess their viability.

data.title: MolecuLight files ITC complaint against importation of infringing bacteria imaging products. Jan. 13, 2026.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on January 13, 2026, MolecuLight Corp. and MolecuLight Inc. revealed a groundbreaking development. These companies, known globally for their advanced fluorescence imaging technology used in real-time identification of harmful bacteria and digital wound measurement, shared news about a recent action. They disclosed filing a complaint with the United States.

data.title: Walker & Dunlop's Apprise names Nelson Pratt as head of new HUD/FHA multifamily division, with a 5-person team.
data.description: In Bethesda, Md., Apprise by Walker & Dunlop has introduced a new national HUD/FHA multifamily valuation and market study practice group. The team will be led by Nelson Pratt, MAI, serving as managing director and national HUD practice lead. Under Pratt's supervision, the group will handle HUD/FHA appraisal and market study activities, enhance quality control protocols, and manage team growth. This specialized team consists of five members and is dedicated to expanding Apprise's HUD-insured multifamily valuation capabilities.

data.title: GM CEO Mary Barra moves company to smaller Detroit HQ for team collaboration amid EV competition. Previous HQ was larger Renaissance Center. CEO emphasizes need for closer collaboration in post-pandemic world.
data.description: Mary Barra, the CEO of General Motors (GM), stated that the company's new headquarters in Detroit aims to promote collaboration among teams. The new HQ, smaller than the previous headquarters at the "Renaissance Center," facilitates better teamwork according to Barra. The previous HQ, established in the 1970s by Ford Motor Co., was located in Detroit as well. Barra expressed that the downsizing would enhance collaboration among the automaker's teams, fostering better connections. The move was planned before remote work became popular due to the pandemic, indicating that the scale of the previous headquarters was not conducive to effective teamwork. The price of GM stock can be checked on the NYSE.

data.title: Uptime Institute predicts data center trends for 2026, focusing on growth and AI impact.
data.description: Uptime Institute recently unveiled its Five Data Center Predictions for 2026 in New York. These predictions delve into the future of the digital infrastructure sector, going beyond the evident trends and addressing key developments and obstacles. The forecast for 2026 highlights the industry's ongoing expansion and associated hurdles, with particular emphasis on the role of AI as a significant catalyst for growth. AI is identified as a crucial factor fueling a substantial wave of investment that will shape the industry in the upcoming years.

data.title: Suzu Labs acquires "Simply Offensive" podcast; Phillip Wylie named host. Suzu enters podcasting, expands media initiatives.
data.description: In Las Vegas, Suzu Labs, a leading cybersecurity and artificial intelligence firm, has recently disclosed their acquisition of the rights to produce the "Simply Offensive" cybersecurity podcast from Simply Cyber Media. This move comes as Simply Cyber Media exits the Podcasting Platform market. The podcast will now be a part of Suzu's growing media and thought leadership ventures, with Phillip Wylie, a renowned cybersecurity expert, serving as the Senior Consultant and Evangelist for Suzu. This acquisition marks a significant step in Suzu's strategy to enhance its presence in the cybersecurity realm.

data.title: Oscar Health, Inc. held its 2025 Q4 and full year earnings conference call in New York.
data.description: In New York, Oscar Health, Inc. is set to host their 2025 Fourth Quarter and Full Year Earnings Conference Call. The call will provide a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance over the past year. Investors and stakeholders will have the opportunity to analyze key data and metrics pertaining to the company's revenue, profitability, and growth. This conference call is crucial for understanding Oscar Health's position in the market and its future prospects.
